description: Runs a sliding median filter (kernel=7) over each channel, then subtracts the filtered signal from the raw signal to produce a residual. The residual isolates sharp transients that the median filter cannot track. The score is the L2 norm of the residual divided by the L2 norm of the filtered signal, giving a transient-to-baseline ratio.
notes: Median filters are nonlinear and preserve edges while removing Gaussian noise. The residual captures exactly what the filter rejected: sharp spikes and transients. The ratio score normalizes by baseline activity so it works across varying impedance conditions.
